<insights>

- Low cohesion (0.28) — files are loosely related, consider splitting this zone
- High coupling (0.72) — 3 imports target "web"

- Serves as the foundational UI layer with heavy dependencies from other zones
- Contains both low-level components (breadcrumb, favicon) and high-level views (architecture, rex-dashboard)
- High coupling suggests this zone may be taking on too many responsibilities
- Zone has very low cohesion (0.28) and high coupling (0.72), indicating poor architectural boundaries
- Foundation anti-pattern: mixing low-level components (breadcrumb, favicon) with high-level application views (rex-dashboard, architecture)
- Cross-domain responsibility: contains rex-specific dashboard view despite being foundational UI layer
- Abstraction level violations: types and constants (appropriate) mixed with complete application views (inappropriate)
- Foundation zone violates single responsibility by containing both infrastructure (types, constants) and application views (rex-dashboard, architecture)
- Acts as dependency hub for multiple zones but lacks clear interface boundaries between foundation utilities and application features
- Application-specific views (architecture.ts, rex-dashboard.ts) are misplaced in foundational layer and belong in domain-specific zones
- Application views architecture.ts and rex-dashboard.ts violate foundation zone boundaries - these domain-specific pages should be moved to appropriate application zones
- Extremely fragile zone with worst-case architectural metrics (0.28 cohesion, 0.72 coupling) requiring immediate intervention
- sourcevision-tabs.ts violates foundation zone naming conventions by including domain-specific prefixes
- Test coverage gaps: only 2 test files for 8 implementation files, indicating potential quality risks
- Zone exhibits catastrophic architectural metrics (0.28/0.72) making it extremely fragile - requires immediate decomposition
- sourcevision-tabs.ts violates foundation naming conventions with domain-specific prefix - rename to generic-tabs.ts or move to domain zone
